programmes.show_description: 1995. Hosted by Antoine de Caunes and Jean Paul Gaultier. EuroTrash takes Japan to explore the Toto Toilet factory, where toilets are scented and heated and meet Japan's favourite weather woman.
2025. Director: Marc Ball. Reflect on how Bruce Lee revolutionised Hollywood, an industry dominated by racial stereotypes. His legacy has redefined roles and paves a new way for Asian actors.
1968. Horror. Directors: Gerardo de Leon & Eddie Romero. Stars John Ashley, Kent Taylor & Mario Montenegro. Tropical island natives must contend with man eating plants and animals!

The Beginning Part 2. 1973. Stars Keir Dullea, Gay Rowan & William Osler. The farmers travel the ship to various cultures in search for someone who can correct the course of the ship and save mankind.
The Plunge of Peril. 1941. Starring: Warren Hull. Brett returns & wants to get to the bottom of the racket. A helpful lead suggests that the crime syndicate has its fingers in more than one pie.
The Magic Book. 1956. Stars William Russell, Ronald Leigh-Hunt & Cyril Smith. Sir Lancelot helps to keep safe an Abbey and the occupants from invaders.
1965. The Princess and The Paupers. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. A pop singer is being held captive for ransom money, but is all as it seems?
1947. Comedy. Directed by Elliott Nugent. Stars Bob Hope, Dorothy Lamour & Peter Lorre. A photographer on death row recounts the events that led to his conviction to a group of reporters.
1947. Action. Director: John Rawlins. Stars Boris Karloff, Ralph Byrd & Anne Gwynne. Dick Tracy goes up against a villain who robs banks using a nerve gas.
1953. Western. Director: Ray Nazarro. Stars Sterling Hayden, Eve Miller, Barton MacLane. An army engineer is sent to oversee the construction of the Kansas Pacific railroad and stop any saboteurs.
1939. Mystery. Director: Thorold Dickinson. Stars Leslie Banks, Greta Gynt, Ian McLean. Poison, death and mystery set at the Arsenal Stadium. Detective Slade is called to solve the crime.
A Bride for Jed. 1964. Stars Buddy Ebsen, Lester Flatt & Joi Lansing. Mr. and Mrs. Drysdale try to help Jed find a wife and they come up with a unique idea with the help of some country musicians.
Ice Spy. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. Mr. Freeze is back in town and he plans to build an icy weapon that will have the world at his control.
1955. Drama. Director: Edward Dmytryk. Stars Deborah Kerr, Van Johnson & John Mills. In WW2 London, a writer falls in love with the wife of a civil servant. Both men suspect her of infidelity.
Groove along to British pop group The Avons and their song Do It With You (I Wanna Do It).
1985. The classic game show Give us a Clue hosted by Michael Parkinson, this week's guests are Jim Bowen, Anna Dawson, Paul Jones, Julia McKenzie, Kenneth Williams & Wincey Willis.
The Fiddlesticks Affair. 1965. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo and Kuryakin plan to blow up a Thrush treasury containing $55 million. The facility's cover is a casino.
Travel to 1964 and take a trip from Oz to London, then to The Rover Factory in Coventry in the 1930s and Mike and Noel have found a brilliant lost reel of The Dave Clark Five.
The Good Samaritan. 1989. Stars Robert Powell, Sharon Maughan & Marc Zuber. The pleasures of travelling on the Trans Europe Express from Venice are often relished! But not for Hannay...
A Brush with the Law. 1983. Stars Mollie Sugden, Christopher Blake & Jennifer Lonsdale. Ida accidentally steals a blouse. She has to attend court where she makes an unexpected friend!
Cross Channel. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, Patrick Holt & Paula Byrne. Ellen and Charles meet in Paris where they plan to kill her husband. Things take a dark turn when Ellen learns she is in danger.
The Case of the Killer's Mark. 1964. Stars John Barrie, William Gaunt & Charles Morgan. A courting couple find the body of a woman in a derelict beach house, her forehead marked with the letter S.
1972. With Freddie Starr and Peter Goodwright, guest acts such as Arthur Mullard, Lance Percival and Johnny More play a number of roles in a series of sketches and songs!
1958. Crime. Directed by Don Siegel. Stars Eli Wallach, Robert Keith & Richard Jaeckel. A ruthless hitman is sent to recover packages of drugs hidden inside the luggage of unsuspecting travellers.

programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart Show with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: A TOTP style TV Show for Heritage Artists based on the Heritage Chart which is voted on in over 80 countries. Songs you love from popular artists, live and on video with the odd interview.
1964. Musical starring John Leyton, Mike Sarne, Freddie & the Dreamers, Ron Moody, Liz Fraser, Nicholas Parsons and Danny La Rue. A group of high-spirited teens find friendship, love & music.
1961. A light-hearted look at the various relaxation methods on offer to people.
1963. Who Killed Sweet Betsy? Directed by Hy Averback. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. One of a set of identical sisters with odd personalities is poisoned with cyanide.
1965. Fringe Benefits. Gig Young, David Niven and Charles Boyer are The Rogues, members of two related families of international forgers and conmen.
The Jongleur. 1956. Stars: Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Rufus Cruickshank. A jongleur apparently on the run from the Sheriff meets up with Robin.
Marooned. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Bill Owen & Peter Hammond. Shortly after capturing Captain Clip West, the tables turn, resulting in Tempest and his men being marooned on an island.
The Golden Wheel. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Having discovered where the resistance hides its funds, Gessler make plans to stop further money reaching them.
1954. Drama. Director: Maurice Elvey. Stars Brian Rix, Prunella Scales & Joan Sims. A newlywed couple with no home are forced to live with their parents and are never permitted a moment's privacy.
1954. Drama. Director: John Guillermin. Stars Joan Rice, Josephine Griffin, Freda Jackson. The story of the ups and downs of a group of salesgirls at a department store in the run up to Christmas.
1953. Crime. Directed by John Guillermin. Stars Guy Rolfe, Lisa Daniely, Patricia Dainton. A surgeon is forced to operate on an injured man by a gang of smugglers.
1958. Drama. Director: Max Varnel. Stars Jack Watling, Sarah Lawson & Robert Raikes. Edgar Mills and his mistress Stella plot to murder Edgar's wealthy wife Clare, but things do not go to plan.
1966. Western. Directed by Monte Hellman. Stars Jack Nicholson, Cameron Mitchell & Millie Perkins. Three cowboys are pursued by a relentless posse after being mistaken for a gang of outlaws.
School for a King. 1962. Stars Dermot Walsh, Peter Illing, David Davies. Determined to learn more about his realms and the plight of his subjects, Richard sets out for the countryside in disguise.
1959. Crime Drama. Director: Max Varnel. Stars Denis Shaw, Kay Callard & Tony Quinn. An Interpol detective pursues a dangerous thief who has placed his loot into a bank account in Rio de Janeiro.
1948. Crime Drama. Director: Ben R. Hart. Stars John Blythe, Wally Patch & Stan Paskin. Two police officers go undercover as husband and wife to expose a smuggling ring operating along the Thames.
1952. Drama. Directed by Robert Parrish. Stars Dana Andrews, Märta Torén & George Sanders. A reporter becomes entangled in a dangerous web when investigating a plot against Hungary's dictatorship.
1968. A fascinating look at the space needed to house the rising population in Britain, including the benefits and problems involved with high rise living.
Cross Channel. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, Patrick Holt & Paula Byrne. Ellen and Charles meet in Paris where they plan to kill her husband. Things take a dark turn when Ellen learns she is in danger.
We ride back to Devon in 1955; a reel takes us to Sunderland in the 70s with Sir Tommy Steele! There's footage of British bandleader Henry Hall and we talk Titch & Quackers.

programmes.show_name: Francis Durbridge's A Game of Murder
programmes.show_description: 1966. Stars Gerald Harper, June Barry & David Burke. Kathy is in hospital after being shot and a flustered wife confesses that she knows who linked to the murders...

programmes.show_name: Play For Today: The Flipside of Dominick Hide
programmes.show_description: 1980. Director: Alan Gibson. Stars Peter Firth, Caroline Langrishe & Pippa Guard. Dominick is a time traveller from Earth's future who is searching for an 'ancestor'.
1971. Crime Thriller. Directed by Sidney Lumet. Starring Martin Balsam, Dyan Cannon, Sean Connery, Christopher Walken. A habitual criminal looks for funding to set up his next robbery.
